description_long: This is a minimalist wooden chair with a gently curved seat and backrest, both made from bent plywood. The seat and backrest are attached to a simple, angular frame with four straight legs, also constructed from wood. The chair features visible screw fastenings on both the seat and backrest, adding a subtle industrial touch to its otherwise natural appearance. The backrest is slightly reclined for ergonomic support, while the seat is wide and flat, providing comfortable sitting space. The wood grain is clearly visible throughout, giving the chair a warm, organic look. There are no armrests, and the overall design is lightweight and functional, suitable for modern urban environments.
description: A minimalist wooden chair with a curved plywood seat and backrest, supported by a simple four-legged wooden frame. The chair has visible screw fastenings and a natural wood grain finish.
description_view_0: Front view shows the wide, curved seat and backrest with visible screw fastenings and the straight, angled legs.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the gentle recline of the backrest and the thickness of the seat and back panels.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the full width of the backrest, the connecting support bar, and the back leg structure.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the profile of the seat and backrest curvature and the leg alignment.
